Control Arm - N.evidence: The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 states: 'Overall, 651 patients were assigned to receive nivolumab plus cabozantinib (323 patients) or sunitinib (328 patients).' The sunitinib arm is the control arm, and it included 328 patients.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: '...we randomly assigned adults with previously untreated clear-cell, advanced renal-cell carcinoma to receive either nivolumab (240 mg every 2 weeks) plus cabozantinib (40 mg once daily) or sunitinib (50 mg once daily for 4 weeks of each 6-week cycle).' This indicates two treatment arms.

Control Arm.evidence: In the 'METHODS' section on page 1, the trial describes assigning patients to 'either nivolumab (240 mg every 2 weeks) plus cabozantinib (40 mg once daily) or sunitinib (50 mg once daily'. The experimental treatment is nivolumab plus cabozantinib, making sunitinib the comparison group or control arm. This is further confirmed in the 'RESULTS' section and Table 1, which lists 'Sunitinib' as one of the treatment arms.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: nivolumab (240 mg every 2 weeks) plus cabozantinib (40 mg once daily)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: '...we randomly assigned adults with previously untreated clear-cell, advanced renal-cell carcinoma to receive either nivolumab (240 mg every 2 weeks) plus cabozantinib (40 mg once daily) or sunitinib (50 mg once daily for 4 weeks of each 6-week cycle).' Treatment Arm 1 refers to the experimental arm, which is nivolumab plus cabozantinib.

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: "...we randomly assigned adults with previously untreated clear-cell, advanced renal-cell carcinoma to receive either nivolumab (240 mg every 2 weeks) plus cabozantinib (40 mg once daily) or sunitinib (50 mg once daily for 4 weeks of each 6-week cycle)." The title of the article also indicates "Nivolumab plus Cabozantinib versus Sunitinib", implying Nivolumab plus Cabozantinib is the experimental arm.

Total Participants - N.evidence: In the 'RESULTS' section on page 1, it states: 'Overall, 651 patients were assigned to receive nivolumab plus cabozantinib (323 patients) or sunitinib (328 patients).' This is further confirmed in the 'PATIENTS AND TREATMENTS' section on page 4: 'Between September 2017 and May 2019, a total of 651 patients underwent randomization at 125 sites in 18 countries; 323 patients made up the intention-to-treat population in the nivolumab-plus-cabozantinib group, and 328 patients made up the intention-to-treat population in the sunitinib group.' The total number of participants is 651.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'At a median follow-up of 18.1 months for overall survival'. This is reiterated on page 4 in the 'EFFICACY' section: 'At a median follow-up for overall survival of 18.1 months (range, 10.6 to 30.6)'.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'Overall, 651 patients were assigned to receive nivolumab plus cabozantinib (323 patients) or sunitinib (328 patients).' The nivolumab plus cabozantinib arm is the experimental arm. This is further confirmed in Table 1 on page 5, which lists 'Nivolumab plus Cabozantinib (N=323)' and 'Sunitinib (N=328)'.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: On page 1, under 'METHODS', it states: 'Health-related quality of life was an exploratory end point.' Under 'RESULTS', it states: 'Patients reported better health-related quality of life with nivolumab plus cabozantinib than with sunitinib.' On page 3, under 'END POINTS AND ASSESSMENTS', it details how 'Health-related quality of life was assessed as an exploratory end point with the use of the National Comprehensive Cancer Network 19-item Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Kidney Symptom Index (FKSI-19)'. On page 9, under the 'QUALITY OF LIFE' section, detailed results are provided, including mean FKSI-19 total scores, and a comparison of quality of life between the treatment groups, concluding that 'Patients had significantly better quality of life with nivolumab plus cabozantinib than with sunitinib at most time points through 91 weeks'.

Quality of Life Scale.value: Health-related quality of life was assessed as an exploratory end point with the use of the National Comprehensive Cancer Network 19-item Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Kidney Symptom Index (FKSI-19; scores range from 0 to 76, with higher scores indicating fewer symptoms) and the 9-item subset of disease-related symptoms (FKSI-DRS; scores range from 0 to 36, with higher scores indicating fewer symptoms). Patients reported better health-related quality of life with nivolumab plus cabozantinib than with sunitinib. The mean (±SD) FKSI-19 total scores at baseline were similar in the two groups (58.7±10.6 with nivolumab plus cabozantinib and 58.4±9.9 with sunitinib). Quality of life was maintained over time with nivolumab plus cabozantinib, whereas a consistent deterioration from baseline was reported with sunitinib. Patients treated with nivolumab plus cabozantinib had better quality of life than those treated with sunitinib at all time points through week 91 (Fig. S5A). Disease-related symptoms as measured by the FKSI-DRS subscale improved from baseline in patients in the nivolumab-plus-cabozantinib group, whereas patients in the sunitinib group had a decline from baseline after week 7 through week 91 (Fig. S5B). The between-group differences were significant (P<0.05) at all time points except week 7 for the FKSI-19 total score and week 79 for the FKSI-DRS score.
Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states that 'Health-related quality of life was an exploratory end point.' The 'END POINTS AND ASSESSMENTS' section on page 3 details the scales used: 'National Comprehensive Cancer Network 19-item Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Kidney Symptom Index (FKSI-19)' and '9-item subset of disease-related symptoms (FKSI-DRS)', including their score ranges and what higher scores indicate. The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 and the 'QUALITY OF LIFE' section on page 9 provide the findings, stating that 'Patients reported better health-related quality of life with nivolumab plus cabozantinib than with sunitinib' and elaborating on the FKSI-19 and FKSI-DRS scores and trends over time, including statistical significance.

Treatment Class.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 and the 'TRIAL DESIGN AND TREATMENTS' section on page 2 describe the treatments as 'nivolumab plus cabozantinib' and 'sunitinib'. Page 2 further clarifies that 'cabozantinib (a small-molecule inhibitor of tyrosine kinases)' and 'nivolumab (a programmed death 1 [PD-1] immune checkpoint inhibitor antibody)' are used. The 'DISCUSSION' section on page 9 explicitly refers to 'Regimens that combine an anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 antibody with a tyrosine kinase inhibitor'.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Found in Table 3, 'Adverse Events (As-Treated Population)', on page 10. Under the 'Nivolumab plus Cabozantinib (N=320)' column, for 'Any event', the 'Grade ≥3' value is 241 (75.3).
Found in Table 3, 'Adverse Events (As-Treated Population)', on page 10. Under the 'Sunitinib (N=320)' column, for 'Any event', the 'Grade ≥3' value is 226 (70.6).

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: Found in the 'Adverse events of any cause during treatment occurred...' paragraph on page 7, which states: '60.6% of the patients in the nivolumab-plus-cabozantinib group and 50.9% in the sunitinib group had treatment-related adverse events of grade 3 or higher (Table S3).' The exact N is not explicitly stated in the provided document, only the percentage.

Found in the 'Adverse events of any cause during treatment occurred...' paragraph on page 7, which states: 'Overall, one death was considered by investigators to be treatment-related with nivolumab plus cabozantinib (small-intestine perforation)'. The total number of patients in the treatment arm is 320 (from Table 3 header), so 1/320 = 0.003125, which is 0.31%.

Found in the 'Adverse events of any cause during treatment occurred...' paragraph on page 7, which states: 'two deaths were considered to be treatment-related with sunitinib (pneumonia and respiratory distress in one patient each)'. The total number of patients in the control arm is 320 (from Table 3 header), so 2/320 = 0.00625, which is 0.63%.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'the median progression-free survival was 16.6 months (95% confi- dence interval [CI], 12.5 to 24.9) with nivolumab plus cabozantinib'. This is also confirmed in Figure 1A on page 6 under 'Nivolumab+Cabozantinib' as '16.6 (12.5-24.9) mo'.
